title: Oxford / Folly Farm, Beckley (Easter); Chesham & Flamstead

main_route.excerpt: We are camping at Folly Farm Beckley, a large windy field on top of a hill with a view across a misty plain.
We ended up outside Chesham at Pedmore in Pednore Bottom, a lovely, small, valley campsite, a cow farm.
We had tea in the van at the stables & set off for home to open Charles' presents.

excursions.label: Walk through Beckley and to Otmoor (Saturday)

stops.excerpt: We walk through Beckley, a lovely pasture woody walk, where there are tripod pheasant feeders.

excursions.label: Drive to Oddington and Beckley Church (Sunday)

stops.excerpt: On Sunday we started early to get to Beckley Church for "9.15. Eucharist with Music"
In the afternoon we drove to Oddington, walked to see the [?Skew Ray] river & looked at the church.

excursions.label: Day visit to Oxford including Dragon School concert and punting (Monday)

stops.excerpt: After that we set off to the Dragon School where we are to pick up Thomas who has been on a week's IAPS course - music. We walked down to look at the Cherwell + punts.

excursions.label: Visit to Flamstead riding stables near Berkhamstead (Tuesday)

stops.excerpt: we found a very good stables Flamstead near Berkhamstead, Ashley Hill. Very kind people.

unresolved.issue: The diary gives the campsite location as 'outside Chesham at Pedmore in Pednore Bottom'. Pedmore is a district of Stourbridge (West Midlands), which seems far from Chesham. This may be a diary misspelling or an alternate local name for a valley near Chesham, Buckinghamshire. Coordinates cannot be confirmed.
unresolved.excerpt: We ended up outside Chesham at Pedmore in Pednore Bottom, a lovely, small, valley campsite, a cow farm.

boundary_flags: mixed_end: page A_0040_L is flagged boundary_mixed=true — the last diary entry shares a page with the start of the next trip.
summary: An Easter 1991 camping trip with six of the children (Thomas joining mid-trip after a music course at the Dragon School, Oxford). The family based themselves at Folly Farm, Beckley (Oxfordshire) for three nights, exploring Otmoor, Oddington and Oxford before moving to a valley campsite near Chesham for a final night. On the way home Tuesday they visited riding stables at Flamstead near Berkhamstead, where Charles rode a Shetland pony on his second birthday.
extraction_notes: Trip is entirely within England; no ferry crossings. Thomas joined the family on Sunday/Monday at Oxford (Dragon School IAPS music course) rather than departing with them on Friday. The date_end of 1991-04-16 (Tuesday) is inferred: Easter 1991 fell on 31 March, so Good Friday was 29 March; April 12 is the following Friday — the family appear to be taking a later Easter break. The page A_0040_L is flagged mixed_end (boundary_mixed=true), meaning the final lines may overlap with the next trip. The campsite spelling 'Pedmore / Pednore Bottom' near Chesham is puzzling — Pedmore is normally in Stourbridge; flagged as unresolved.
